assertion_results.text: The post-triage summary comment (Step 7) includes an @mention of the vulnerability issue's reporter using an ADF mention node with account ID '557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id' (§1.67)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md (Step 7 Post-Triage Summary Comment ADF), lines 278-289 contain an ADF mention node: {"type": "mention", "attrs": {"id": "557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id", "text": "@psirt-analyst"}}. The account ID matches exactly.

assertion_results.text: The reporter @mention in Step 7 is present by default without any ProdSec configuration — it uses the reporter field from the Jira issue (§1.67)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md line 42, the output states: 'It includes a mandatory @mention of the reporter (psirt-analyst, account ID 557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id) using an ADF mention node.' The word 'mandatory' indicates it is always present by default. The account ID '557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id' matches the Reporter field extracted in data-extraction.md line 45: 'Reporter | psirt-analyst (account ID: 557058:psirt-analyst-mock-id)', confirming it is sourced from the Jira issue's reporter field, not from ProdSec configuration.

assertion_results.text: The Affects Versions correction comment (Step 3) includes an @mention of the ProdSec contact using an ADF mention node with account ID '557058:prodsec-mock-account-id' (§1.68)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md (Step 3 Affects Versions Correction Comment ADF), lines 148-157 contain an ADF mention node: {"type": "mention", "attrs": {"id": "557058:prodsec-mock-account-id", "text": "@prodsec-team"}}. The account ID matches exactly.

assertion_results.text: The ProdSec @mention appears before the Comment Footnote in the Affects Versions correction comment (§1.68)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the ADF mention node for ProdSec is at lines 148-157, followed by the rule (horizontal divider) at lines 158-160, and then the Comment Footnote paragraph ('This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/triage-security v0.11.0.') at lines 161-185. The @mention clearly precedes the footnote in the document structure.

assertion_results.text: Step 0 extracts the ProdSec Jira account ID from the Security Configuration as an optional field without raising an error (Step 0 config validation)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md line 15, the ProdSec Jira account ID is listed as: '**ProdSec Jira account ID** _(optional)_: 557058:prodsec-mock-account-id'. The field is explicitly marked as '(optional)' and was successfully extracted with the correct value. No error is mentioned anywhere in the Step 0 configuration validation output.
